samples.xml_description:
<module id="div_16bit">
    <description>Design a simplified radix-2 divider on 8-bit numbers.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst">Reset signal.</input>
        <input id="sign">Signed or unsigned operation.</input>
        <input id="dividend" width_description="[15:0]">Input operand DIVIDEND.</input>
        <input id="divisor" width_description="[15:0]">Input operand DIVISOR.</input>
        <input id="opn_valid">Valid operation request.</input>
        <input id="res_ready">The output has been acknowledged.</input>
        <output id="res_valid">The result is valid and ready.</output>
        <output id="result" width_description="[15:0]">Output result.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <param id="IDLE">0</param>
        <param id="SUB">1</param>
        <param id="SEL">2</param>
        <param id="SHIFT">3</param>
        <param id="DONE">4</param>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <sequential_logic id="div_valid">
            <assign>
                <at> rst </at>
                <true> 1'b0 </true>
                <false> opn_valid </false>
            </assign>
            <next_state>
                <at> rst </at>
                <true> 1'b0 </true>
                <false> opn_valid </false>
            </next_state>
        </sequential_logic>
        <sequential_logic id="cnt">
            <assign>
                <at> rst </at>
                <true> 4'b0 </true>
                <false> opn_valid ? 4'b1 : cnt </false>
            </assign>
            <next_state>
                <at> rst </at>
                <true> 4'b0 </true>
                <false> opn_valid ? 4'b1 : (cnt & 4'b1111) </false>
            </next_state>
        </sequential_logic>
        <sequential_logic id="sr">
            <assign>
                <at> rst </at>
                <true> 16'b0 </true>
                <false> opn_valid ? {dividend, 8'b0} : sr </false>
            </assign>
            <next_state>
                <at> rst </at>
                <true> 16'b0 </true>
                <false> opn_valid ? {dividend, 8'b0} : sr </false>
            </next_state>
        </sequential_logic>
        <sequential_logic id="neg_divisor">
            <assign>
                <at> rst </at>
                <true> 16'b0 </true>
                <false> opn_valid ? {divisor, 8'b0} : neg_divisor </false>
            </assign>
            <next_state>
                <at> rst </at>
                <true> 16'b0 </true>
                <false> opn_valid ? {divisor, 8'b0} : neg_divisor </false>
            </next_state>
        </sequential_logic>
        <sequential_logic id="neg_divisor">
            <assign>
                <at> rst </at>
                <true> 16'b0 </true>
                <false> opn_valid ? {divisor, 8'b0} : neg_divisor </false>
            </assign>
            <next_state>
                <at> rst </at>
                <true> 16'b0 </true>
                <false> opn_valid ? {divisor, 8'b0} : neg_divisor </false>
            </next_state>
        </sequential_logic>
        <combinational_logic id="sub_result">
            <assign>
                <at> rst </at>
                <true> 16'b0 </true>
                <false> opn_valid ? sr - neg_divisor : 16'b0 </false>
            </assign>
        </combinational_logic>
        <combinational_logic id="mux_sel">
            <assign>
                <at> rst </at>
                <true> 1'b0 </true>
                <false> opn_valid ? ~sub_result[15] : 1'b0 </false>
            </assign>
        </combinational_logic>
        <combinational_logic id="mux">
            <assign>
                <at> rst </at>
                <true> 16'b0 </true>
                <false> opn_valid ? mux_sel ? sub_result : sr </false>
            </assign>
        </combinational_logic>
        <combinational_logic id="shift">
            <assign>
                <at> rst </at>
                <true> 16'b0 </true>
                <false> opn_valid ? {mux, sr[15:1]} : sr </false>
            </assign>
        </combinational_logic>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
    The module performs division operation when opn_valid is high and the operation is not finished(res_valid is low).
    The division operation is done by shifting the shift register and the quotient and remainder are stored in the shift register.
    The division operation is done by 8 steps and the counter is used to control the steps.
    The remainder is stored in the upper 8 bits of the shift register and the quotient is stored in the lower 8 bits of the shift register.
    The division operation is done when the counter is not 8 and the shift register is updated by the subtraction result or the previous value.
    The result is valid when the counter is 8 and the division operation is done.
    The result is valid when res_valid and res_ready is high, it means that the result is consumed and the result is not ready anymore.
    </implementation>
</module>
